name: org.machanism.machai:genai-client
description: GenAI Client is a Java library for integrating application code with generative AI and embedding providers through stable, vendor-neutral contracts. It provides the org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai interface for conversational generation, prompt accumulation, system instructions, state clearing, input logging, tool registration, resource registration, and provider execution. It provides org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.EmbeddingProvider for semantic embedding generation. Concrete adapters include OpenAIProvider for OpenAI-compatible Responses and embedding APIs, AnthropicProvider for Anthropic Claude Beta Messages API, CodeMieProvider for EPAM CodeMie OAuth 2.0 authentication with downstream provider delegation, and ToolsProvider for deterministic host-side YAML tool invocation. The library also includes annotation-driven tool metadata with Tool, Prompt, Resource, Param, SupportedFor, FunctionTools, FunctionToolsLoader, ToolFunction, ParamDescriptor, Role, and SpecialException. It supports MCP server configuration, optional web search tools, function calling, prompt and resource discovery by reflection, Java ServiceLoader-based tool installation, configurable error handling, request timeout configuration, usage accounting with Usage and UsageStatistics, and runtime provider resolution with GenaiProviderManager using Provider:Model strings. Primary use cases include AI-powered Java applications, semantic search, similarity comparison, automated content generation, model-assisted project assembly, provider abstraction, tool-aware AI workflows, usage tracking, and integration with OpenAI, Anthropic Claude, EPAM CodeMie, OpenAI-compatible endpoints, MCP servers, and host-defined Java function tools.

usageContext: Java applications that need a common GenAI provider API
Host-defined function tools exposed to AI providers
MCP server and web-search-enabled generation workflows

constructors.description: Creates an OpenAI-backed Genai and EmbeddingProvider implementation. Initialize it with a model identifier and a org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator containing OPENAI_API_KEY and optional OPENAI_BASE_URL, GENAI_TIMEOUT, MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS, MAX_TOOL_CALLS, WebSearchTool.*, and MCP.* settings before calling prompt, perform, or embedding.

examples.description: Creates and runs an OpenAI provider. The configurator must provide OPENAI_API_KEY and may provide OPENAI_BASE_URL for OpenAI-compatible endpoints. The provider can be reused after clear().

constructors.description: Creates an Anthropic-backed implementation of the Genai abstraction. Configure it with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, a Claude chat model, and optional ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL, GENAI_TIMEOUT, MAX_OUTPUT_TOKENS, MAX_TOOL_CALLS, and cacheThreshold settings before generation.

examples.description: Creates and runs an Anthropic Claude provider using the Anthropic Java SDK. The configurator must provide ANTHROPIC_API_KEY.

constructors.description: Creates a CodeMie provider that obtains an OAuth 2.0 access token from EPAM CodeMie and delegates to a downstream OpenAIProvider or AnthropicProvider depending on the model prefix. Configure GENAI_USERNAME, GENAI_PASSWORD, BASE_URL, optional AUTH_URL, and the requested model.

examples.description: Authenticates to CodeMie, configures a downstream OpenAI-compatible provider, and performs a generation request. Use a username containing @ for password grant or a service client id for client credentials grant.

constructors.description: Creates a local tool-only provider. The constructor disables conversational error handling so tool execution exceptions are propagated fail-fast as SpecialException. In yaml mode, the last prompt is parsed as a YAML tool-call descriptor and the matching registered Java ToolFunction is invoked.

examples.description: Creates a deterministic tool execution provider. Register FunctionTools first, then pass a YAML prompt naming the tool and parameters to execute locally.

features.name: getProvider
features.signature: public static org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai getProvider(java.lang.String chatModel, org.machanism.macha.core.commons.configurator.Configurator conf)
features.description: Dynamically loads and initializes a Genai provider from a Provider:Model string. If the provider segment contains a dot, it is treated as a fully qualified class name; otherwise the conventional provider implementation package is used. Returns null for a blank provider name and throws IllegalArgumentException when provider resolution, instantiation, or type matching fails.

examples.description: Resolves and runs a chat provider without directly depending on the concrete implementation class. This is the recommended factory-style workflow for application code.

features.name: getEmbeddingProvider
features.description: Dynamically loads and initializes an EmbeddingProvider from a Provider:Model string. The returned provider can compute numeric vectors for text, useful for semantic search and similarity comparison.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.EmbeddingProvider embedder = org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenaiProviderManager.getEmbeddingProvider("OpenAI:text-embedding-3-small", configurator);
java.util.List<java.lang.Double> vector = embedder.embedding("customer support knowledge base article", 1536L);
examples.description: Loads an embedding provider and requests an embedding vector for text. Configure provider credentials in the configurator before calling this method.

features.name: perform
features.description: Executes the provider to produce a response from accumulated prompts, system instructions, tools, resources, MCP servers, and provider state. Concrete providers may handle tool-call loops and usage capture internally before returning the final text response.

examples.description: Adds instructions and a prompt, then invokes the configured provider to obtain a generated response.

features.name: embedding
features.signature: public abstract java.util.List<java.lang.Double> embedding(java.lang.String text, long dimensions)
features.description: Computes an embedding vector for input text using a configured embedding-capable provider such as OpenAIProvider or CodeMieProvider. Returns null when supported implementations receive null text.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.EmbeddingProvider provider = org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenaiProviderManager.getEmbeddingProvider("OpenAI:text-embedding-3-small", configurator);
java.util.List<java.lang.Double> vector = provider.embedding("semantic search query", 1536L);
examples.description: Generates an embedding vector that can be stored in a vector index for semantic search or similarity matching.

features.name: addTools
features.description: Registers annotated Java tool methods from a FunctionTools instance. AbstractAIProvider scans public methods annotated with org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.Tool, extracts names, descriptions, and parameter metadata, and delegates concrete registration to the provider implementation.

examples.description: Registers an application-specific FunctionTools implementation so model tool calls can invoke host Java methods during generation.

features.name: applyTools
features.description: Discovers FunctionTools implementations with Java ServiceLoader and applies compatible tools and prompts to a Genai provider. Compatibility may be restricted by the SupportedFor annotation on FunctionTools implementations.

examples.description: Installs ServiceLoader-discovered tools into a provider for a specific application class. Add provider declarations under META-INF/services/org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools in dependent jars.

features.name: addUsage
features.signature: public static void addUsage(java.lang.String modelId, org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.Usage usage)
features.description: Records a token usage entry for a model identifier in the synchronized UsageStatistics registry. Usage records capture input tokens, cached input tokens, and output tokens for one provider interaction.

examples.description: Records and logs model token usage for operational reporting and cost diagnostics.

customizations.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools
customizations.description: Implement this SPI to bundle host-defined tools, prompts, and resources. Annotate methods with Tool, Prompt, Resource, and Param metadata, then expose the implementation through Java ServiceLoader for automatic discovery by FunctionToolsLoader.

examples.description: Defines an installable tool set. Register the class in META-INF/services/org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools so FunctionToolsLoader can discover it.

customizations.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.AbstractAIProvider
customizations.description: Extend this abstract base class to implement a new provider adapter. Subclasses inherit configuration handling, MCP and web-search bootstrap logic, input logging, annotation-driven tool discovery, configurable error handling, timeout handling, working-directory propagation, and safe tool invocation utilities. Implement the abstract addTool hook and provider-specific perform behavior.

examples.description: Shows the minimum shape of a custom provider adapter using the shared AbstractAIProvider infrastructure.

customizations.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.GenaiAdapter
customizations.description: Use or subclass this delegating adapter to add cross-cutting behavior such as logging, metrics, retries, request shaping, or authentication before forwarding calls to an underlying Genai provider. Call setProvider before invoking delegated methods.

examples.description: Wraps a concrete provider behind the same Genai contract. GenaiAdapter is not thread-safe unless the delegate is thread-safe and external synchronization is used.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.Genai
studs.description: Primary provider boundary for conversational and tool-enabled generative AI. It defines initialization, prompts, instructions, perform, clear, input logging, tool registration, prompt registration, resource registration, project directory propagation, error handling, and enabled tool selection.

examples.description: Uses the Genai interface to remain independent of concrete vendor SDK classes.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.EmbeddingProvider
studs.description: Embedding provider boundary for implementations that initialize with configuration and compute numeric vectors from text. Use it for semantic search, similarity comparison, and other embedding-based NLP workflows.

examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.EmbeddingProvider provider = org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenaiProviderManager.getEmbeddingProvider("OpenAI:text-embedding-3-small", configurator);
java.util.List<java.lang.Double> vector = provider.embedding("example text", 1536L);
examples.description: Uses the EmbeddingProvider abstraction to generate vectors without depending on a concrete SDK.

studs.name: org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.ToolFunction
studs.description: Functional interface representing a provider-callable host tool. Implementations receive structured com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.JsonNode parameters and runtime context objects such as java.io.File working directories or configurators, and return a tool result object.

examples.description: Implements a tool with a lambda expression. Exceptions thrown from apply are handled according to the provider error-handling mode.

examples.title: Install the Maven dependency
examples.scenario: Add GenAI Client to a Maven application so code can use the Genai, EmbeddingProvider, provider implementations, and tool annotations.
steps: Configure a repository that contains org.machanism.machai artifacts if it is not already available in your build environment.
Add the Maven dependency to pom.xml.
Provide provider credentials with your application configuration mechanism before creating providers.
examples.snippet:
<dependency>
  <groupId>org.machanism.machai</groupId>
  <artifactId>genai-client</artifactId>
  <version>1.2.2-SNAPSHOT</version>
</dependency>
examples.description: This installs the library in a Maven project. The artifact targets Java 8 or newer. Runtime credentials such as OPENAI_API_KEY or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY are supplied through the Configurator used by provider initialization.

examples.title: Configure and run an OpenAI chat request
examples.scenario: Use the manager factory and a Provider:Model string to create a provider, configure prompts, run generation, and clear state.
steps: Ensure the configurator contains OPENAI_API_KEY.
Resolve the provider with org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenaiProviderManager.getProvider.
Set system instructions and one or more prompts.
Call perform to execute the request.
Call clear before reusing the provider for an unrelated conversation.
examples.description: This practical scenario runs a real chat completion through the common Genai abstraction. Swap OpenAI:gpt-4o-mini for another supported Provider:Model string to change backends.

examples.title: Generate embeddings for semantic search
examples.scenario: Create an embedding provider and transform text into a vector for indexing or similarity comparison.
steps: Configure credentials for an embedding-capable provider such as OpenAI or CodeMie.
Resolve the embedding provider with GenaiProviderManager.getEmbeddingProvider.
Call embedding with text and the desired provider-specific vector dimensionality.
Store the returned java.util.List<java.lang.Double> in a vector database or compare it with other vectors.
examples.snippet:
org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.EmbeddingProvider embedder = org.machanism.machai.ai.manager.GenaiProviderManager.getEmbeddingProvider("OpenAI:text-embedding-3-small", configurator);
java.util.List<java.lang.Double> vector = embedder.embedding("How to configure MCP tools", 1536L);
examples.description: Embedding generation is useful for semantic search, retrieval augmented generation, clustering, recommendations, and similarity matching.

examples.title: Install and invoke host-defined tools
examples.scenario: Expose Java methods as AI-callable tools using annotations and ServiceLoader-compatible FunctionTools implementations.
steps: Create a class that implements org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools.
Annotate public methods with org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.Tool and parameters with org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.Param.
Register the class in META-INF/services/org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools for ServiceLoader discovery, or pass the instance directly to provider.addTools.
Create a Genai provider and apply tools with FunctionToolsLoader or addTools.
Prompt the model to use the available tools when needed.
examples.description: This scenario demonstrates direct tool installation. For automatic installation, add a service provider file named META-INF/services/org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionTools containing com.example.ProjectTools and call new org.machanism.machai.ai.tools.FunctionToolsLoader().applyTools(provider, com.example.MyApplication.class).

examples.title: Run deterministic YAML tool execution
examples.scenario: Use ToolsProvider as a local tool executor when no remote LLM call is needed and the last prompt is a YAML tool call descriptor.
steps: Create org.machanism.machai.ai.provider.impl.ToolsProvider.
Initialize it with the yaml model.
Register FunctionTools with the provider.
Pass a YAML prompt containing tool and params.
Call perform to invoke the matching local tool.
examples.description: ToolsProvider is useful for deterministic workflows and tests. It is fail-fast by default, so exceptions from tool execution are propagated instead of converted into conversational text.
